title: Development of a Gamification Model for Personalized E-Learning
abstract: This thesis develops a gamification model to personalize e-learning experiences, applying game-design elements to improve learner engagement and outcomes within digital learning platforms.

title: Architectural Model for Wireless Peer-to-Peer (WP2P) File Sharing for Ubiquitous Mobile Devices
abstract: This paper presents an architectural model for wireless peer-to-peer file sharing among mobile devices, using a hybrid semi-centralized architecture where a central database server mediates between devices, and SMS/MMS serve as the messaging medium for file discovery and transfer over GPRS-based 2/2.5G networks.

title: An Improved Anomalous Intrusion Detection Model
abstract: This study addresses class imbalance in network traffic data to improve anomalous intrusion detection accuracy, using live traffic captured under normal conditions and various attack scenarios to reduce false positives and improve detection of minority-class attacks.

title: Breast Cancer Risk Prediction Using Data Mining Classification Techniques
abstract: This study applies Naive Bayes and J48 decision tree classification techniques to predict breast cancer risk among Nigerian patients, comparing model accuracy and error rates to identify the more effective approach for early risk detection.

title: Bridging the Gap in Modern Computing Infrastructures: Issues and Challenges of Data Warehousing and Cloud Computing
abstract: This paper examines how data warehousing and cloud computing can work together to support informed decision-making, reviewing OLAP and data mining techniques built on data warehouse models and discussing integration challenges.

title: Development of an Online Shopping Mart System for Developing Countries
abstract: This study develops EasyShop, a web-based online shopping mart system for developing countries, built with ASP.NET/C# and MySQL, designed to eliminate the need for physical buyer-seller contact and support flexible, remote shopping transactions.

title: A Noun-Centric Keyphrase Extraction Model: Graph-Based Approach
abstract: This study proposes a graph-based keyphrase extraction model incorporating noun-phrase identification, combining TextRank, affinity propagation clustering, and k-means clustering, evaluated against an existing model using precision, recall, and F-measure.
keywords: keyphrase extraction,natural language processing,graph-based algorithms,text mining

title: Performance Evaluation of Neural Network MLP and ANFIS Models for Weather Forecasting Studies
abstract: This study uses Adaptive Neuro-Fuzzy Inference System (ANFIS) and Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP) neural network models to analyze meteorological data covering a ten-year period, finding ANFIS yielded better prediction results than MLP for weather forecasting.

title: Network Threat Characterization in Multiple Intrusion Perspectives Using Data Mining Technique
abstract: This paper presents a threat characterization model for network attacks combining Frequent Temporal Sequence Association Mining and Fuzzy Logic, using Apriori Association Mining to detect temporal rule patterns and rate exploits from both victim and attacker perspectives.

title: Characterisation of Academic Journal Publications Using Text Mining Techniques
abstract: This study develops text mining techniques to characterise academic journal publications, using Journal Quality List rankings as selection criteria and Self-Organising Maps to cluster highly-rated journals based on extracted bibliometric features.

title: Improving Information Acquisition via Text Mining for Efficient E-Governance
abstract: This paper proposes a framework integrating text mining with e-governance, allowing users to describe interests in text that are processed through clustering and term extraction to generate relevant content, tested on several websites.

title: Language Identification Using Deep Convolutional Recurrent Neural Networks
abstract: This paper proposes a language identification system that operates on spectrogram images rather than raw audio, using a hybrid Convolutional Recurrent Neural Network shown to be robust across noisy scenarios and extendable to unseen languages.

title: A Systematic Review of Information Retrieval and Ranking Algorithms
abstract: This paper conducts a systematic review of information retrieval, search, and ranking algorithms, providing a theoretical framework and identifying research gaps to serve as a basis for developing an enhanced recursive model for individualized search.

title: An Enhanced Algorithm for Ranking Results Based on User Specified Traits
abstract: This paper develops the Enhanced Recursive Ranking Algorithm (ERRA), allowing users to specify search criteria in order of relative importance for ranking people, publications, and records across the web and social networks.

title: Towards the Development of an Enhanced Recursive Model for Individualized Search
abstract: This paper develops an enhanced recursive ranking model to improve individualized search experience by incorporating user biases, addressing limitations of the QuickRank Algorithm in handling evolving user preferences.

title: Research on Internet Addiction: A Peep into the Future
abstract: This review explores conceptual and terminological issues surrounding Internet addiction, reviewing thirty-two studies from 2007 to 2012 and proposing a clearer conceptualization of internet addiction measurement using set theory.

title: Hate Speech Analysis Using Machine Learning
abstract: This paper examines machine learning approaches to hate speech detection, addressing challenges posed by users obfuscating text with slang and inventive spellings that evade current word-embedding-based detection methods.
keywords: hate speech,machine learning,natural language processing,text classification

title: Circular Gabor Wavelet Algorithm for Fingerprint Liveness Detection
abstract: This paper presents a fake-fingerprint identification algorithm using Circular Gabor Wavelet for texture segmentation, achieving 99.968% accuracy in distinguishing live from spoof fingerprints made of materials like gelatin and play-doh.

title: Development of a Web Application for Birth and Death Certificate Request
abstract: This study develops a web-based application for requesting birth and death certificates from Nigeria's National Population Commission, built with MySQL, PHP, and Bootstrap, aiming to reduce cost and stress compared to the manual process.

title: Prevalence and Determinants of Internet Addiction Among Adolescents
abstract: This study surveys 1,022 university adolescents using the Revised Internet Addiction Test to investigate internet addiction prevalence, finding a 3.3% prevalence rate and that addiction was mainly driven by extrinsic rather than intrinsic motivations.

title: A Data Mining-Based Response Model for Target Selection in Direct Marketing
abstract: This study builds a predictive response model using Naive Bayes to identify customers likely to respond to promotions at a Nigerian microfinance bank, using historical purchase data stored in a data warehouse for decision support.

title: The Adoption of a National Cloud Framework for Healthcare Delivery in Nigeria
abstract: This paper proposes a National Cloud Framework model for adopting eHealth into government-owned cloud computing infrastructure in Nigeria, addressing healthcare sector challenges and proposing adoption strategies for other developing countries.

title: Comparative Analysis of the K-Nearest Neighbour and the Gaussian Naive Bayes Algorithms on the KDDCup'99 Benchmark Dataset
abstract: This study compares K-Nearest Neighbor and Gaussian Naive Bayes algorithms on the KDDCup'99 intrusion detection dataset, finding KNN achieved 99.8% accuracy but a much longer prediction time than Gaussian Naive Bayes on large datasets.

title: Technological Development and Human Security in Nigeria
abstract: This paper examines the role of technology, including surveillance systems and biometric identification, in addressing multi-dimensional insecurity challenges in Nigeria, arguing for a collaborative approach prioritizing human security needs.

title: The Future of Biometric Technology: Vein and Odor Recognition
abstract: This paper explores emerging biometric technologies, particularly vein pattern and odor recognition, as methods for accurately authenticating individuals and securing computer systems beyond conventional fingerprint and iris methods.

title: Brain Tumour Detection and Classification Using Vision Transformer Neural Networks
abstract: This study proposes a vision transformer neural network system to detect and classify brain tumours from MRI images, trained and validated on data from a Nigerian hospital, achieving 95.58% accuracy in distinguishing glioma, meningioma, and pituitary tumours.
keywords: brain tumour detection,vision transformers,medical imaging,deep learning

title: The Role of Cyber Hygiene Practices in the Digital Era: Case Studies from Nigerian Public Service
abstract: This paper examines cyber hygiene practices across Nigerian government institutions, identifying gaps in cybersecurity awareness and outdated policies, and recommends strategies to improve resilience against cyberattacks in the public sector.

title: Interrogating the Security and Privacy of Digital Identity in the Nigerian Civil Service: Challenges and Opportunities
abstract: This paper examines digital identity management within Nigeria's civil service, exploring security and privacy risks of digital system adoption and proposing tailored cybersecurity frameworks and data protection strategies for the public sector.

title: A Design and Implementation of a Full Stack Web Application in Identifying and Responding to Cyber Threats
abstract: This project develops a full-stack web application to help small and medium-sized Nigerian businesses identify and respond to cyber threats, featuring real-time threat updates, an IOC analyser, email analyser, and CVSS calculator, built with React, Node.js, and PHP Laravel.

title: A Framework for Data Sovereignty and AI Governance in Digital Twin-Enabled Upstream Oil & Gas Systems in Africa
abstract: This paper proposes the Sovereign AI & Digital Twin Governance model, integrating data sovereignty, ethical AI controls, and digital twin interoperability, validated through case analysis of upstream oil and gas operations in Nigeria and Angola.
keywords: AI governance,digital twin,data sovereignty,oil and gas

title: Cybersecurity of AI-Driven Adaptive Threat Systems: A Framework for Resilient Defense Against Autonomous Adversarial Malware
abstract: This paper introduces the Adaptive Cyber Defense Framework, a conceptual model integrating resilient architecture and adaptive detection mechanisms to defend against AI-driven, autonomous adversarial malware that evades traditional static defenses.

title: Enhancing Business Decision Making Through Actionable Knowledge Discovery Using a Hybridized MCDM Model
abstract: This paper proposes a hybridized interval type-2 fuzzy Multi Criteria Decision Making model using Fuzzy AHP and a Compensatory AND approach to rank data mining patterns by unexpectedness, actionability, and novelty for business decision support.

title: ANOSPEX: A Stochastic, Spatially Explicit Model for Studying Anopheles Metapopulation Dynamics
authors: Oluwagbemi OO, Fornadel CM, Adebiyi EF, Norris DE, Rasgon JL
abstract: This paper presents a C++-based stochastic spatially explicit model to simulate Anopheles mosquito metapopulation dynamics, parameterized by field data from Zambia, useful for evaluating malaria control strategies.

title: MACBenAbim: A Multi-Platform Mobile Application for Searching Keyterms in Computational Biology and Bioinformatics
abstract: This paper describes MACBenAbim, a flexible mobile application for searching, defining, and describing keyterms in computational biology and bioinformatics, aimed at supporting students and researchers in developing countries.

title: Development of a Prototype Hybrid-Grid-Based Computing Framework for Accessing Bioinformatics Databases and Resources
abstract: This paper develops a hybrid-grid-based computing framework for bioinformatics, proposing a system architecture for maintaining, sharing, and expanding bioinformatics knowledge in geographically distributed environments.

title: Promoting Public Health and Safety: A Predictive Modeling Software Analysis on Perceived Road Fatality Contributory Factors
abstract: This study uses Multilayer Perceptron predictive modeling software to analyze the relationship between perceived road fatality factors and public health impacts across European countries using road fatality data from 1970-2005.

title: Building a Computer-Based Expert System for Malaria Environmental Diagnosis: An Alternative Malaria Control Strategy
abstract: This paper presents a malaria environmental diagnosis expert system built with Java and the Java Expert System Shell, providing decision support to researchers and healthcare practitioners in malaria-endemic regions.

title: PQ Trees, Consecutive Ones Problem and Applications
abstract: This research article examines PQ trees and the Consecutive Ones Problem, demonstrating their operations through simulation and developing an analyzer tool useful for clustering software in bioinformatics applications.

title: Evaluating the Relationship Between Running Times and DNA Sequence Sizes Using a Generic-Based Filtering Program
abstract: This research article studies the relationship between running times of a generic-based filtering program and genetic sequence sample sizes, finding the complexity of the generic tree program to be O(log2 N).

title: Towards Developing Grid Based Portals for E-Commerce on Demand Services on a Utility Computing Platform
abstract: This paper investigates the requirements for effective grid-enabled portal design for utility computing, developing and evaluating a prototype for the GUISET utility computing initiative for small and medium enterprises.

title: Towards a Well-Secured Electronic Health Record in the Health Cloud
abstract: This technical report presents an encryption-based electronic health record security model addressing data security and privacy concerns in cloud-based healthcare implementations, highlighting three core functionalities for managing eHR privacy and security.

title: An Analysis of Scripting Languages for Research in Applied Computing
abstract: This paper presents a comparative study of ten popular scripting languages used in software engineering, bioinformatics, and computational biology, evaluating them against selected criteria to guide researchers in choosing appropriate languages.

title: BengaSaVex: A New Computational Genetic Sequence Extraction Tool for DNA Repeats
abstract: This research develops BengaSaVex, a computational tool for extracting repeated DNA nucleotide subsequences from gene sequences of microorganisms, and compares its performance against existing tools like tandem repeat finder.

title: Sentiment Analysis and Text Classification of Nigerian Social Media Data Using Machine Learning
abstract: This thesis investigates sentiment analysis and text classification of Nigerian social media data using machine learning algorithms including Naive Bayes, SVM, and Random Forest, addressing unique challenges posed by code-mixing between English and Yoruba, Igbo, and Hausa in social media text. The study builds a labelled corpus of 12,000 tweets and achieves 87.4% classification accuracy with an ensemble model.
keywords: text mining,sentiment analysis,machine learning,natural language processing,social media,classification

title: Development of a Machine Learning-Based Malaria Diagnosis Support System for Rural Health Centres
abstract: This study develops a malaria diagnosis support system using decision tree and random forest classifiers trained on patient clinical features collected from rural health centres in Osun State, Nigeria. The system achieves 92.1% sensitivity and 89.7% specificity, aiding non-specialist healthcare workers in early malaria detection and reducing diagnostic turnaround time in resource-limited clinical settings.
keywords: healthcare,malaria diagnosis,machine learning,decision tree,medical informatics,random forest

title: An Adaptive Query Expansion Framework for Improving Information Retrieval in Academic Digital Libraries
abstract: This dissertation proposes an adaptive query expansion framework that uses pseudo-relevance feedback and word embeddings to improve information retrieval performance in Nigerian academic digital libraries. The framework demonstrates significant improvements in precision and recall over baseline BM25 retrieval, and includes a ranking module that re-scores results using contextual term co-occurrence patterns extracted from the local corpus.
keywords: information retrieval,query expansion,academic digital libraries,ranking,search engines,pseudo-relevance feedback

title: Design and Implementation of a Mobile-Responsive Alumni Tracking Web Application for Nigerian Universities
abstract: This project designs and implements a mobile-responsive alumni tracking web application for Obafemi Awolowo University using React, Django REST Framework, and PostgreSQL. The web application enables alumni to update professional profiles, search for peers by industry and graduation year, and connect with current students for mentorship, featuring secure OAuth2 authentication and a RESTful API backend.
keywords: web application,alumni tracking,full-stack development,React,database systems,REST API

title: Evaluation of Retrieval Models for Scientific Literature Search in Sub-Saharan African Repositories
abstract: This study evaluates TF-IDF, BM25, and neural retrieval models for scientific literature search across Sub-Saharan African academic repositories. The paper proposes a hybrid ranking model that balances term frequency weighting with semantic similarity to improve document discovery, reporting MAP, NDCG, and Precision@k scores on a curated benchmark of 4,200 indexed papers drawn from Nigerian and Ghanaian institutional repositories.

title: A Deep Learning Framework for Real-Time Network Intrusion Detection and Cyber Threat Classification
abstract: This thesis develops a deep learning framework using Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks and Convolutional Neural Networks for real-time network intrusion detection and cyber threat classification. The model is trained on the CICIDS2017 benchmark dataset and validated on live campus network traffic at Obafemi Awolowo University, achieving 98.3% detection accuracy with a false positive rate below 0.8% across seven attack categories.

title: Predictive Analytics for Early Detection of Hypertension Using Electronic Health Records in Nigerian Tertiary Hospitals
abstract: This dissertation applies predictive analytics and ensemble machine learning methods to electronic health records from three Nigerian tertiary hospitals to identify early hypertension risk factors. Using gradient boosting, XGBoost, and stacking classifiers on a dataset of 18,500 patient records, the study achieves 91.3% classification accuracy and highlights age, BMI, salt intake, and family history as the most predictive clinical features for proactive healthcare intervention in resource-limited settings.
keywords: healthcare,predictive analytics,hypertension,electronic health records,machine learning,medical diagnosis

title: Blockchain-Based Academic Certificate Verification System for Nigerian Universities
abstract: This project designs and prototypes a blockchain-based certificate verification system to combat academic credential fraud in Nigeria. Using Ethereum smart contracts and the IPFS distributed file system, the system stores cryptographic hashes of degree certificates issued by Nigerian universities in a tamper-proof public ledger, enabling instant verification by employers and postgraduate institutions via a lightweight web portal.

title: An IoT-Based Smart Campus Energy Monitoring and Management System
abstract: This project develops an Internet of Things (IoT)-based energy monitoring system for university campuses using ESP32 microcontrollers, the MQTT protocol, and a React-based real-time dashboard. Deployed across five buildings at Obafemi Awolowo University, the system visualises energy consumption patterns, detects anomalous usage, and triggers automated relay-controlled load shedding, reducing average building energy waste by an estimated 18%.

title: Interactive Data Visualization Dashboard for COVID-19 Epidemiological Trends in Nigeria
abstract: This study develops an interactive web-based dashboard for visualising COVID-19 epidemiological trends across Nigeria's 36 states using D3.js and Python Flask. The dashboard enables public health officials to monitor confirmed case counts, vaccination rates, and mortality patterns through dynamic choropleth maps, time-series charts, and statistical summary panels updated from the NCDC open data API.

title: Development of a Named Entity Recognition System for the Yoruba Language Using Transfer Learning
abstract: This thesis develops a named entity recognition (NER) system for the Yoruba language by fine-tuning a multilingual BERT model on a manually annotated Yoruba news corpus comprising 8,400 sentences. The model achieves an F1 score of 78.6% on a held-out test set and the thesis contributes the first publicly available Yoruba NER benchmark dataset, addressing a critical gap in African language natural language processing resources.
keywords: natural language processing,Yoruba language,named entity recognition,BERT,transfer learning,text mining

title: A Microservices Architecture for Scalable E-Learning Platforms in Nigerian Higher Institutions
abstract: This dissertation proposes and evaluates a microservices-based architecture for building scalable e-learning platforms tailored to Nigerian higher institutions. Contrasting it with monolithic approaches, the study demonstrates improved fault tolerance, horizontal scalability, and deployment flexibility using Docker and Kubernetes, and reports 99.2% uptime and a 3.1x throughput improvement under simulated peak load conditions representative of university examination periods.
